methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $250.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $400M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
xAI, a Series E AI & Software company, is valued at $250B post-money. The comparable universe consists of 9 private frontier-tech peers (Crusoe Energy, Groq, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, WEKA, and three others) and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), all operating in the AI & Software sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $250B last post-money valuation, applies a 1.4x typical Series E step-up multiplier, yielding an implied value of $350B. Peer-median analysis across the six same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $4.7B, with a range spanning $400M (low) to $10B (high, Crusoe Energy). The peer cohort exhibits substantial dispersion: the 25th percentile sits near $1.6B (WEKA) while the 75th percentile approaches $7B (Groq).

Triangulation of both methods produces a range of $4.0B (low) to $402.5B (high), with a median of $177.3B. The wide spread reflects a fundamental tension: stage-progression methodology anchored to the last round supports valuations in the $250–350B range, while peer comparables suggest Series E AI companies typically command $4–7B valuations. Key uncertainties include the degree to which xAI's scale, capital raised ($42B), and founder backing justify a valuation multiple orders of magnitude above sector peers, and whether peer comparables remain relevant given the outlier position of the target.
